findings.description: Heading hierarchy and text emphasis are reduced in the HTML, making the title and certification/adoption statements less visually distinct.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the document title is very large and prominent, and the adoption/certification lines are displayed in large text blocks. In the HTML, these appear much smaller and closer to body-text size, reducing visual distinction though the content remains present.

findings.description: List formatting is preserved semantically but rendered with weaker visual indentation and emphasis.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the amendment content as a clearly indented bulleted list with a nested square bullet and larger blue text. The HTML keeps bullets and nesting, but the text is smaller, darker, and less visually separated from surrounding content.
